name: Lemon, Chocolate and Berry Roulade
recipeIngredient: 125 grams caster sugar (plus extra for dusting)
225 grams mixed berries (such as redcurrants, Tayberries, raspberries, strawberries) - stalks removed, hulled, and halved if necessary
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F/Gas 6. Lightly oil and line a 23 x 33 cm Swiss roll tin with non-stick baking paper.
2. Whisk together the eggs, caster sugar, and vanilla essence in an electric whisk for 5 minutes until thick and frothy. The whisk should leave a trail. Gradually fold in the coconut and lemon rind. Pour into the tin and bake for 12-15 minutes until firm. Allow to cool.
3. Place a sheet of non-stick baking paper on a flat surface, matching the size of the tin. Sprinkle with extra caster sugar. Turn the cooled roulade onto the paper and carefully peel off the lining paper. Trim the edges of the roulade.
4. Drizzle lemon juice over the roulade. Melt the chocolate in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of simmering water, then spread it evenly over the roulade and top with fresh berries. Use the baking paper to help roll up the roulade. Decorate with reserved cream, additional fresh berries, and sprigs of mint. Dust with icing sugar before serving.
To freeze: Freeze at the end of stage 2 for up to 3 months.
